# CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryDataTypeIsBaseDataType(_:)

**Function**

Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether a data type identifier represents a base data type.

## Declaration

```swift
func CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryDataTypeIsBaseDataType(_ dataType: CFString) -> Bool
```

## Parameters

- **dataType**: Identifier of the data type to be queried.

## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/kCFBooleanTrue] if first data type conforms to the second data type; [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/kCFBooleanFalse] otherwise.

## Discussion

This is simply a convenience method to test to see if a given data type identifier is in the array returned by [doc://com.apple.coremedia/documentation/CoreMedia/CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryGetBaseDataTypes()].

## Inspecting Metadata

- **CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryDataTypeIsRegistered(_:)**: Returns a Boolean value that indicates the registration status of a data type identifier.
- **CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryGetDataTypeDescription(_:)**: Returns the data type description if it exists.
- **CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryGetConformingDataTypes(_:)**: Returns the conforming data types for the data type, if any.
- **CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryDataTypeConformsToDataType(_:conformsTo:)**: Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether a data type conforms to another data type.
- **CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryGetBaseDataTypeForConformingDataType(_:)**: Returns the base data type identifier that a data type conforms to.
- **CMMetadataDataTypeRegistryGetBaseDataTypes()**: Returns an array of base data type identifiers.
